title: Criteria Air Pollutants Source-to-Impact Tracker
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive vis-network diagram tracing six EPA criteria air pollutants from their sources through atmospheric chemistry to health and environmental impacts.

educational.topic: Criteria Air Pollutants and Atmospheric Pollution
learningObjectives: Trace each criteria air pollutant from its source through atmospheric chemistry to health and environmental impacts
Explain how pollutants share common sources and interact with each other
Distinguish between primary and secondary pollutants
Analyze why reducing emissions from a single source can have multiple environmental benefits
prerequisites: Introduction to the Clean Air Act and EPA

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Network visualization with interactive path tracing
keyQuestions: Which pollution source connects to the most criteria pollutants?
How does ground-level ozone form as a secondary pollutant?
If a city closes its coal-fired power plant, which impacts would be reduced?
Why is air pollution better understood as a system than a list of chemicals?
commonMisconceptions: Each air pollutant comes from a single unique source
All criteria pollutants are emitted directly into the air (ignoring secondary formation)
Reducing one pollutant only affects one health or environmental outcome
assessmentOpportunities: Path-tracing exercise from source to impact demonstrates systems understanding
Comparison of cascade effects when different sources are eliminated
Written analysis of why the Clean Air Act regulates these six specific pollutants
